disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of a scraping, burning throat sensation and a feeling of something stuck, triggered the moment dry crackers were eaten into a very dry mouth
trigger_question_examples: What happened and when did it start?
What were you doing when it came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was eating dry crackers for dinner and suddenly my throat felt like the food scraped and stuck — it's burning and my mouth is bone dry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to swallow water comfortably in small sips without choking or coughing
trigger_question_examples: Can you swallow water or fluids?
Are you able to drink at all right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I can still sip water and get it down okay — it's the dry food that got stuck, not liquids.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No drooling and able to manage own saliva
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to swallow your own spit, or is it pooling and making you drool?
Do you feel like saliva is building up that you can't swallow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not drooling — I can swallow my own spit fine, my mouth is just really dry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No difficulty breathing, no noisy breathing and no sensation of the throat closing over
trigger_question_examples: Is your breathing okay?
Does it feel like your throat is closing or your breathing is noisy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My breathing is fine — it's not noisy and my throat doesn't feel like it's closing, it's just dry and burning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain localised high in the throat and back of the mouth, not behind the breastbone
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly does it feel stuck or sore?
Is the pain up in your throat or lower down in your chest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's high up in my throat and the back of my mouth — not down in my chest behind the breastbone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing dry eyes and dry mouth (sicca symptoms) over about a year
trigger_question_examples: Have your mouth and eyes been dry for a while?
Any ongoing health conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My eyes and mouth have been really dry for about a year — I use eye drops. I was told my blood test was positive for Sjögren's.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors or feeling systemically unwell (temperature not actually measured)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a fever or shaking chills?
Do you feel unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't feel feverish and I haven't had the shakes, but I haven't actually checked my temperature.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No swelling, lump or tenderness of the cheek or under the jaw (parotid/submandibular region)
trigger_question_examples: Is your cheek or the area under your jaw swollen or sore?
Any painful lump in front of your ear or under your jaw?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my cheeks and jaw aren't swollen or sore — there's no lump that I can feel.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No white coating in the mouth and no painful cracks at the corners of the lips
trigger_question_examples: Is there any white coating on your tongue or cheeks?
Any painful cracks at the corners of your lips?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No white coating and no cracks at my lips — it's just very dry and red-feeling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Frightened that something is physically lodged in the throat requiring hospital
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried about?
Is there something specific you're afraid this could be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm scared there's a piece of cracker actually stuck in my throat and that I'll need to go to hospital tonight.
